examples.title: Basic waitpid() Usage
examples.description: Simple example showing waitpid() to wait for a specific child
examples.explanation: Parent forks a child, waits for it using waitpid() with pid>0 (specific child), then extracts exit code using WEXITSTATUS after confirming normal exit with WIFEXITED.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is child execution time

examples.title: Non-blocking waitpid() with WNOHANG
examples.description: Using WNOHANG to poll for multiple child terminations
examples.explanation: Uses WNOHANG to poll - returns 0 if no child exited, >0 when child reaped. Allows parent to do other work while waiting for children to finish.

examples.title: Waiting for Process Group
examples.description: Using waitpid() with process group options
examples.explanation: Demonstrates pid = -getpid() to wait for process group members. The negative pid value tells waitpid() to wait for any child in that specific process group.

examples.title: Checking Multiple Status Conditions
examples.description: Properly checking all possible termination scenarios
examples.explanation: Shows comprehensive status checking using all macros. The child calls abort() which generates SIGABRT, so WIFSIGNALED will be true and WTERMSIG will return SIGABRT value.
